audit_items.proof_trace: `resolv_query()` (resolv.c:203-208) calls `query_free_cb(query)` to free the `query_t` object when all DNS submissions fail, but then falls through to `return cb_data` instead of `return NULL`, leaving `server->query` as a non-NULL dangling pointer to freed memory. When any subsequent `close_and_free_server` call fires, `resolv_cancel` reads `client_free_cb`/`client_cb_data` from the already-freed `cb_data` (UAF) and invokes `query_free_cb(query)` a second time, producing a double-free of the same `query_t` allocation.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker floods the shadowsocks-libev server with connection requests requiring DNS resolution, exhausting the udns query pool so that `dns_submit_a4` (and/or `dns_submit_a6`) returns NULL; the dangling `server->query` pointer then causes a double-free heap corruption when the connection is subsequently timed out or closed, enabling heap exploitation.
